sentences.text: Me.
Hey, how's it going?
Not bad, how are you?
Pretty good.
Okay, this event is October, right?
Sorry, what was that?
This event is October.
October, the dates that he gave me were between the 20th and 30th.
Okay.
So, I had just reached out to him, following the conversation, we were like, we need to re-engage.
Remember, Nashville was around this time last year, and he said, yeah, we're doing that again, so…
Are you putting KKR in last month?
Totally up to you.
You probably won't.
Yeah.
One hurt.
Alright, let's do that.
I just let Krista know.
Hmm… Okay, it is during an SGE event, but I think that's fine.
Okay.
do we… here, I guess… here's my question.
Obviously,
They have the big request of property exports and ownership data.
Yep.
Is that something we want to release at the event?
Yeah, that would make sense, right?
maybe, maybe we tell Nate today, hey, we've got it on Alpha, we're looking at it, we're probably…
A few weeks away, so maybe we connect prior, but… but how would you feel about us?
you know, showing it to the team at the event.
Yeah.
Do you want me to give them a walkthrough today?
Do you think that makes sense?
It's pretty casual, you just ask to connect, like, regarding the event, so…
What do you… I'm trying to… what did you read into Alex back and forth on it?
What did I read into?
Yeah, I'm trying to think, does he want us to show that, or does he want to have a set of the look back at it, meeting on it?
Property… I mean, it might not be bad to just review it with Nate today and see if he has feedback on it.
Okay.
I think my read on it was that he's gonna want two things, which is A the ability to save markets, and then B, the ability to automatically schedule that… that list.
Like, if you think about his workflow, if we can do both of those things for him, it's gonna take a… And you can trust that it's the right properties that are landing in that export.

Yeah, I guess it's up to you if you want to give a peek here this morning, if it feels appropriate, but I think we'd want to have a Proper meeting with the rest of the group when it's totally ready, and then… yeah, and maybe that's in person, so…
Sounds good.
Figured as much on Fogel, man, what's going on there?
There's a… Let's talk about that one after… after this.
Okay.
And then, Yeah, so we're just, I assume, just aligning on schedules and details, and I assume… Will he be giving us an hour again, or…
Into?
That would be my… that'd be my guess.
Okay.
I'm wondering if today will be a budget, like, a budget ask as well.
That was kind of my assumption.
Hmm.
Don't feel like we're in a very strong position there all of a sudden.
What's that?
with.
with the Cushman Client Services team.
Yeah, I mean, do we want to ask about that?
Any… just general updates from him on how things are being structured out?
And we could probably just say, hey, how's it?
I wasn't naive, like, how's it going with HelloData?
Yeah.
Yeah, how are… how are you, kind of?
What's… what share of time?
Are they getting compared, you know, that type of thing, maybe?
Yeah.
He's in the… he's in the waiting room.
I'll let him move.
What's up, Nate?
Hey guys, how we doing?
Better than ever.
Hear that.
I just thought of this, Matthew.
We had an all-company event two weeks ago, and Matthew won worst Zoom background, so…
Oh, man, that's tough.
You seemed a little taken aback by that.
I like it.
It's unique, it's, interesting, there's a story behind every portrait behind you, so…
Yeah, that's nice.
I've known our CEO for, I think, 12 years, Nate, and he always has… he can't just say something nice, there's gotta be a slap hug.
Back then.
Yeah, exactly, so…
Yeah, that's good.
But we're good, how are you?
I'm doing well, yeah.
Just, yeah, keeping on, you know, in the… in the ever-changing world that is Cushman and Wakefield right now, so…
Yeah.
Things have been good, though.
Did y'all do, y'all do anything for Labor Day this past weekend?
I mean, my garage, if that counts.
Nice.
And committed to an engagement ring, so it was, It's a grown-up weekend for me, Nate, you know?
Very, yeah, very mature of you.
I know, there usually would have been, like, some daydreaming or something involved.
I was just all about my business.
Yeah, that's good.
if you really want to talk about growing up, I've got, twin boys, Nate, who just started college, so they had a soccer game.
They're college soccer players, so soccer game, on Monday night, and I, cooked for them on Sunday night, which is something I don't really know how to do.
I did their laundry, and then watched their game, so…
Nice.
that doesn't.
good.
Where are they at in the tool?
University of Southern Indiana?
Okay.
Pretty random.
Yeah.
when you're twins and you want to play Division I soccer together, it's not totally your choice, so we'll.
didn't.
That's where they ended up, so…
That's cool.
Yeah.
Yeah, yeah, yeah.
Well, I know we got your event coming up, which we're excited about.
Yeah, I think that was the main reason for the call.
The biggest thing, well, one.
you know, a kind of short, short timeline now.

We finally, you know, got it approved and are good to go, in August, or not August, October 20th, we'll be here, you know, very shortly, but the biggest thing is I wanted to understand, you know, in the past, y'all have sponsored this meeting for us, right?
And, like, what does that mean?
Like, what have you contributed, and how does that work, is my biggest question.
I think last time, honestly, Avery was just really excited about the product, and wanted to get us out in front of you, so I don't think there was anything official.
Okay.
I think we, like, offered to buy dinner, and Woody got his credit card in first.
I don't even know that there was anything kind of formal.
We did dinner.
We bought dinner.
Okay.
Yeah, I mean.
But…
We're open to whatever you think, Nate, but yeah, last time we… we covered the dinner, we covered the drinks afterwards, there wasn't really much more to it than that, obviously.
Okay.
But you tell us, I guess, what are you thinking, or what would be ideal?
Yeah, I just… I wasn't sure if there was something formal, or if there was any sort of standing agreement, but yeah, I think something similar would be perfect.
I think we're gonna have, we're gonna have some clients involved this year, On, the first night, so Tuesday night would probably be the day.
I don't know how long, if y'all wanted to fly in for just Tuesday, or what exactly you wanted to do, but, Tuesday, we're gonna… is gonna be more of our office day, right?
We're gonna be in the San Diego office throughout the majority of the day.
We're gonna have kind of our strategy and, you know, whatever goals conversation in the morning.
We're gonna have a client roundtable, in the afternoon, and then we're gonna transition into, you know, a dinner with those clients.
Okay.
on Tuesday night that we would love to have you guys, be a part of, if that's, if you guys were up for that, so…
For sure.
Yeah, I think that sounds… that sounds awesome.
And then, would you anticipate… I know we had about an hour with… with the team in office last time, is that something that seems…
Yeah, we can, we can, we can carve out an hour on, on Tuesday, for sure, to kind of go over.
what's changed in the tool, and I think, the team has changed, right?
We brought on a new person, even 2 weeks ago, I think it's maybe been.
And then we had Corinne earlier this year, and then even Genevieve, who is… not even been here since, you know, I don't know, February, maybe?
March?
So, another just refresher, and then updates on the, whatever's new in the tool would be… would be great within that hour.
And I think we… we were thinking, like, we'll have, for sure by then, and probably sooner… sorry, Matthew, I just jumped… jumped in here, but
Ownership data is already in the tool, like, on our environment, free exports, occupancy filters, everything.
I think most of what you've been requesting, Nate, so…
Yup.
It's… we can kind of defer to you.
I think we could get the team together prior, and show this to you, and…
Nope.
get it live, but it might also be a nice thing to kind of go through at the event.
So, how much urgency, I guess, on your… what's your preference?
Well, I know we had a, we had a call yesterday as a team, and there was some… desire, to connect back with you all, around the tool.

and I had asked the team that they had, you know, there were certain questions, and I said, hey, I'll consolidate whatever you guys have, whether it's requests for functionality, or questions about current functionality, you know.
send it over, I'll centralize it, and then send it over to y'all.
Haven't gotten anything yet, but, you know… we'll see what happens there.
But yeah, I think there is a desire from the team to Kind of get in front of you guys again.
That can happen before or at the… at the meeting, either way, it's fine with me.

Maybe we can react based on the questions or the feedback, so it sounds like maybe it's better to get that on the calendar sooner rather than later, and then when we're… when we're in person, we could… Hopefully show how we've reacted to your feedback, and show… show some… So, that'd be good.
Cool.
Absolutely.
I know you mentioned Genevieve, sorry I missed everyone in Vegas.
I know Hello Date has infiltrated the team a little bit, Nate, so how's… how's it going?
I'm curious what share they're getting compared to… tell us whatever you're open to.
So Genevieve was the only one… I had… I still don't know what hell of data is, I've never been in it, I've never seen it.
She actually might have sent me a report at one time to look at it.
Got it.
I'm loyal.
If you guys got me, I'm all in.
I don't have an IQ, but I think she's settled down on the Hello Data push, so I haven't heard anything about it, but yeah.
Okay.
I think we're good.
We've had a consensus conversation.
I left that evening going, oh no, I was a little too defensive.
I have too many mad hands with Nate before this.
And then, next morning, Brian sent me, like, a selfie of me and Genevieve, and I was like, I think we're alright.
You guys reconciled.
It was a conversation that ended in friendship, so it's…
Yeah.
Well, I'm looking forward to meeting her.

It's funny, when I… when we saw her name on that invite, it's like… like, it's a big industry, we have a thousand customers, we have, you know, so many conversations, and then I'm like, oh my goodness.
Like, there's a very short list of, like.
People where you're like, oh boy, here we go, so we knew her preference.
From the last couple of years, so… it's good that it sounds like the team is still kind of on board.
with us, and I'd love to hear what Genevieve thinks now that she's kind of seen both more extensively, but…
Yeah, yeah, absolutely.

So I hope everybody sends over any feedback or ideas that they've got around the tool, and if not, I'll follow up with them next week to try to kind of get that over to y'all before, so we have something to… to work towards, or at least discuss, other than whatever's new within.
Cool.
So…
Yeah, I think you guys will be excited.
I don't want to put words in your mouth, Nate, but I think we're pretty close to just the amount of times we've met on your processes and what you all are looking for.
I think we're… Like, we're within an earshot of it, so…
Yeah.
Excited.
The ownership data's gonna be huge when that's in there.
I think that's a big, big push.

And then… at least one thing that I know about that my team has… when I say my team, the analyst team has Kind of pointed out as a pain point the last couple weeks is that the export from the tool has been changing.
In terms of the format of the rows, what's located where.

So I don't know if that's just as things are rolled out, or if that's something that y'all are fine-tuning, but it… it causes problems in our process, because we take that, format it into our underwriting package before sending it out to the client, right?
So as those rows change.
The formulas that we have within our underwriting tool.
It impacts that, right?
That makes sense.
I'm assuming it's the… we did this split where we went calculated metrics versus, observed metrics, essentially.
But that was where the reordering…
Okay.
Perfect.

Yeah, as long as… as long as we've got something that's going to be standard now, you know, that's fine, we can adjust to it, but as… if it's going to continue to adjust, it would be good to know, so that we can… Be prepared for it.
Good feedback.
That's good feedback.
Sorry about that.
Okay.
Goodbye.
Obviously, there'll be changes and updates at some point ongoing, but giving you advance notice.
Nope.
Would be helpful.
Okay.
Yeah, just as long as it ends up being standardized and is one way going forward, right?
Obviously, I know y'all are always… Releasing new functionality and always changing, which is, you know, the great part about the tool as well, but…
Good.
those… some of those little formatting changes impact us, right, in our process, so…
Yeah, that's good to know, Nate, because from your target property process, we're really excited to show you that.

The property list export out of Explore, that's something I think we can all get really excited about, but It's good feedback knowing that moving… moving forward, if any changes are made to that format, that's, you guys are routing that through to something, so…
Yeah.
Yep.
And we've… I guess this… this isn't… this is… kind of has two… Different purposes, but… We've moved away from our target list process because that was a big push from Avery.
However, we mainly moved away from it because it was a large lift for us to put it together, right?
If that becomes an easier lift, if it's more automated, and…
you know, the data that y'all are able to put together for us allows us to put those together easier, or is more streamlined than we would go back to it, right?

And we do do… we still do them on and off, but it is less… Less of a, a focus from a business development perspective on our team, just because, one, it was a huge push by Avery, but two, it was a large lift for my team to put those together every month, right?

So, I'd love to see, once… once y'all have that up and running, if that's something that we can easily pick back up without, you know, with some additional efficiency that allows us to put them together easier than what we had through CoStar in the past.
And I know you shared what… you've… you don't need to repeat yourself.
I know you've shared your thoughts in the past, but if you can just refresh me on what… what's difficult within that process with CoStar?
Like, what… why is it so inefficient?
The biggest problem with CoStar was just the data itself being out-date, right?
Or out of date.
So, a lot of the time, we'd go in there, we have this… Well, one, in CoStar, we can only pull 500 properties at a time, right?

So if we're identifying a specific market, say, for example, Atlanta, we want to look at all the properties in Atlanta, we want to pull 2,500 properties, we have to do, you know, 5 different polls, right?
5 different 500, property total pulls.
Plug that into an Excel sheet that we have that has formulas to identify which properties are not meeting the market average for concessions or vacancy, right?
And then from there, we start to figure out, okay, who are we going to target, who are we going to reach out to, you know, about management?

And then the problem is, we've gone through that whole process just to find out that the data that we have from CoStar is not accurate, because it was updated 6 months ago, and the property isn't actually underperforming anymore, right?
So…
True.
That was the… that's been the issue with that process.
Makes sense.
So…
Okay, accuracy, and then just the limitation on the number of properties that you can export at a given time.
No, no.
helpful.
Well, we can certainly address the accuracy.
We'll check in.
Again, it's… it's brand new.
We'll check in just on the limits, and may have some thoughts.
Thoughts buy you in between now and when we reconnect, but…
Yep.
Anything from… I know you're waiting for their feedback.
Anything just top of mind from the team that we should be thinking about that you remember from the meeting?
I still think finding properties is somewhat problematic.
There's times where it's hard to track down the property that we're looking for.
And then also, I think the comps that are being selected continues to not be, Exactly.
what we… I think more often than not, we adjust the comp set.
Okay.
So, like, that's still… Still, still, going on, but… Yep.
I guess we could look at what we recommended, like, in our back end, and then look at the changes that you made, Nate.
I wonder if…

Yeah, so we could probably do, like, hey, let's pull 10 properties and say, hey, this is the concept that we put together, this is the concept that was automatically pulled, and figure out why, you know, what's the biggest difference?
Right.
Within the island.
It'll probably be something simple, like we're over-indexing on distance or something, you know, something in comparison to how you're looking at it.
Yeah.
Nothing needed from you there.
I think we can actually look at, your actions in the back end, and I'll let you know if we can't, and then we may ask you for, like, a before and after snapshot or something, so…
Yeah, yeah, yeah, no, that'd be great.
Awesome.
Well, back to the event.
If you need something formal, let us know, otherwise we're good to cover everything on that.

reasonable expenses on that Tuesday night, so… Yeah, just dinner on Tuesday night would be great, and I can, I can give you guys more details, I can send you the hotel we're staying at, kind of… I'll give you the full agenda of what our, What our plan is when we're gonna be there, when everybody's getting in, when everybody's leaving, if that helps, and, you know, tag along for as long as you want, or, you know, yeah.
Up to you.
That'd be great.
And then that'll…
No, sorry, go ahead, Corey.
I was gonna say, that'll help us decide who to bring for support as well.

We may bring a VP on our product team that's… that's pretty, helped us kind of speed up some of these requests, and so yeah, any… anything that you can share regarding who will be there would be great.
And then, remember, Matthew, did we pick the restaurant in Nashville, or did Avery…
I was just gonna ask, if you… if you want us to handle that, and A, we've got an events team here, so we just need, like, the number of attendees, we can get that all scheduled.
If you want to handle it, just have us pay, that's fine too.

Yeah, I'll, I'll double check, and see if we've got anything that's, that's been decided, somewhere near the office, and the office is technically in La Jolla, I think, so somewhere around there is what we were aiming for.
So, I'll see if there's something that's being worked on.
If not, we can coordinate accordingly.
Perfect.
That's the size of the group and the desired time-ish, and we'll… we'll take care of it.
Otherwise, if you got a spot, let us know.
Sweet.
Sounds good, guys.
Looking forward to it.
Pleasure.
India, absolutely.
I'll follow up, Nate, in terms of scheduling, just to get the group together to have a new look at Explore and discuss the other product request.
Start with the questions, we can do that, get some on the calendar from there.
Yeah, sounds like a plan.
Appreciate it.
Cool.
Thanks, guys.
See ya.
